programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Snowball Effect/ One Krab's Trash
programme.desc: Pat and SpongeBob play in the snow; Mr. Krabs sells trash as antiques.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Grandma's Kisses/ Squidville
programme.desc: Bob's Grandma gives him a kiss in public; Squid decides to leave.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Good Neighbors/ Skill Crane
programme.desc: Squidward obsesses over winning a stuffed animal; Squid's security system backfires.

programme.title: LEGO City Adventures: Harl Hubbs Day/ Ann They're Off
programme.desc: It's Harl Hubbs Day and Harl decides to help the whole city, but quickly discovers he wasn't prepared for Harl mania. Billy's mother, Ann, accepts a junkyard race challenge against the city's street racing legend, Tread Octane.

programme.title: Power Rangers Beast Morphers: Boxed In
programme.desc: The Pan Global Games have arrived in Coral Harbor, but Ranger duties prevents the teens from attending any of the events.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Mermaidman and Barnacle Boy/ Pickles
programme.desc: Bob and Pat's favourite superheroes are in a retirement home; Bob loses self-confidence.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Patchy's Playlist
programme.desc: Patchy takes a fun-filled journey through the world of SpongeBob's best musical moments.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Eek, an Urchin!/ Squid's Defense
programme.desc: Squidward wants SpongeBob and Sandy to teach him karate after being robbed of his groceries. SpongeBob goes to great lengths to force out a pesky sea urchin from the Krusty Krab.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: High Sea Diving/ Bottle Burglars
programme.desc: SpongeBob wants to be the first sponge to dive all the way up to the surface of the ocean. When SpongeBob Squidward accidentally let Plankton steal the secret formula, it's up to them to steal it back.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Sanctuary! / What's Eating Patrick?
programme.desc: When stray snails start appearing around Bikini Bottom, SpongeBob decides to care for them himself. To defend Bikini Bottom's honor, Patrick enters a Krabby Patty eating competition.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Whale Watching/ Krusty Kleaners
programme.desc: Mr. Krabs hires Squidward to babysit Pearl for the night, but she just wants to sneak out to a cool teen party. SpongeBob spills a milkshake during a delivery.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Call the Cops/ Surf N' Turf
programme.desc: Plankton goes to jail, but the secret formula is taken as evidence so SpongeBob and Mr. Krabs plot to get it back. Sandy struggles to build a ship-in-a-bottle before a big contest, so SpongeBob calls in a little extra help.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: SpongeBob Appreciation Day: Patchy's Beach Bash!
programme.desc: A shipwreck survivor washes up on the Bikini Atoll, where Patchy shows him his favorite SpongeBob moments.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Mind the Gap/ Dirty Bubble Returns
programme.desc: SpongeBob has a change in demeanor after Squidward closes the gap in his teeth.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: A Cabin in the Kelp/ The Hankering
programme.desc: The Gal Pals spend the weekend in a cabin in the woods.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Sandy's Nutty Nieces/ Insecurity Guards
programme.desc: SpongeBob gets in over his head when he volunteers to babysit Sandy's three nieces. SpongeBob and Patrick volunteer as guards at the museum, while Squidward attempts to hang his painting unnoticed.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Monster Who Came to Bikini Bottom/ Welcome to the Bikini Bottom Triangle
programme.desc: A monster terrorizes Bikini Bottom until Patrick befriends the huge creature.

programme.title: Ollie's Pack: Charmed and Dangerous/ Science Unfair
programme.desc: Ollie and the gang save VP Magna from a beautiful monster. Ollie summons a monster to help Bernie with the science fair.

programme.title: The Loud House: House of Lies/ Game Boys
programme.desc: After witnessing an excess of lying in the family, Lisa invents lie-detecting glasses to try and curb the fibs. Lincoln asks to borrow Clyde's new gaming system, but Clyde struggles to trust that it's safe with him.

programme.title: The Loud House: Fool's Paradise/ Job Insecurity
programme.desc: With prankster Luan away at camp for April Fool's Day, the Louds think they should be safe. The kids discover Dad isn't at his old job anymore and worry it might be their fault.

programme.title: World's Wildest Weather: Golden State Nightmare
programme.desc: We hear about the horrifying impact of the Thomas Fires in Southern California in 2017.

programme.title: Strangest Weather on Earth: Fireballs from Space
programme.desc: Meteor shock, armies of snow monsters, blood red falls and tumbleweed invasions are all captured on video, examined and explained.

programme.title: Strangest Weather on Earth: Weird Waters
programme.desc: Giant blooms of glowing creatures, a lake changed into a huge bowl of cement, 60-foot snow drifts turns a journey into a destination, the wind sinks a city and Spider Lightning on this episode of Strangest Weather on Earth.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: The Nutcracker Sweet
programme.desc: Weather reporters get a serious case of the giggles, and some people get really dirty…in the mud.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: When It Rains It Pours
programme.desc: A bikini model slips on the runway, a boater slips overboard, and a weather person has an embarrassing slip of the tongue.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: Forecast: Clear With a Chance of Funny
programme.desc: A heartfelt tribute to some snowmen who sadly melted way too soon, a squirrel goes nuts and makes off with a GoPro, and a guy on a swing takes a hit where the sun don’t shine.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: Camping Can Be In-Tents
programme.desc: People go camping in the not-so-great outdoors, water activities end in epic wipeouts, and summer vacationers have unexpected encounters with wild animals.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: The Most Blunder-full Time of Year
programme.desc: An inflatable reindeer gets frisky with a reporter, a couple of speedboaters go way overboard, and a jockey has an embarrassing wardrobe malfunction.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: The Passing Wind
programme.desc: Weather reporters find trouble when they leave the studio, people do crazy things in the heat, and the wind proves it can literally blow you away.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: Pool-itically Incorrect
programme.desc: Outdoor BBQ plans go up in flames, people literally find themselves on thin ice, and a sunny day at the pool isn't as relaxing as you think.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: Snow Means Snow
programme.desc: A weather man has an unexpected run-in with a rooster, a musical tribute to people slipping on ice, and Santa makes a surprise entrance.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: Ship Happens
programme.desc: It's lights, camera, disaster for local meterologists, couples have second thoughts when their wedding day goes awry, and bad weather rocks the boat for weekend sailors.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: Snow and Tell
programme.desc: A weathercaster has an embarrassing slip of the tongue, a kid uses the sled and string method to pull a loose tooth, and an actor creates his own wind on live TV.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: It's a Wind Wind Situation
programme.desc: A bug makes an emergency landing in a weather reporter’s mouth, an excited dog takes his owner for a drag, and a paragliding groom crashes his own wedding.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: Fair Weather Friends
programme.desc: People find thrills and chills at the county fair, weathercasters experience unexpected interruptions and crazy ways to beat the heat.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: It's Been Nice Snowing You
programme.desc: A skier hits the slopes…with his face, a monkey does his monkey 'business' on a girl’s back, and a girl’s pole dance brings down the house…literally.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: Wind, Lose, or Draw!
programme.desc: A paraglider doesn't know which end is up, a little girl uses some adult words when shoveling snow, and Triumph the Insult Comic Dog delivers a weather report as only he can.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: Just Let It Slide
programme.desc: Skidding cars perform an unexpected ice ballet, a monkey picks a reporter's nose, and a bikini girl on a zipline goes airborne.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: Snow Way
programme.desc: People discover it's not so easy to go dashing through the snow, field reporters realize they should have stayed in the studio and bears come out of hibernation and get into trouble.

programme.title: Ice Pilots NWT: Meltdown

programme.title: Ice Pilots NWT: North Atlantic Convoy

programme.title: Hoarders: Ingrid
programme.desc: Home shopping junky, Ingrid, has let her hoarding take over her home. Unopened boxes fill rooms to the point that it is impossible to use almost the entire house.

programme.title: Hoarders: Al/ Julie
programme.desc: Al's hoarding has him facing $2,500 a day in fines by the county. Julie's hoarding is so bad, no one realized a homeless woman was living among the mounds of stuff in her basement.

programme.title: Hoarders: Roy/ Loretta
programme.desc: Roy is facing $20 million in fines from the county for hoarding hundreds of vehicles on his property. After being robbed at gunpoint, Loretta started to hoard everything in her home.

programme.title: Hoarders: Randy/ Vicki
programme.desc: Randy's boardwalk memorabilia collection fills a 20,000 square foot building and numerous tractor trailers. Vicki's hoarding is breaking up her family.

programme.title: Hoarders: Judy/ Jerry
programme.desc: Judy hoarded herself out of her house and used cancer surgery as an excuse to stay with a friend. Jerry hoarded himself out of a lovely half million-dollar property in a desirable neighbourhood.

programme.title: Movie: Gods of Egypt
programme.desc: In ancient Egypt, a mortal man makes an alliance with the god Horus to take back the throne from Set, god of darkness.

programme.title: Movie: The Mummy Returns
programme.desc: A 3,000-year-old mummy is resurrected and resumes its evil quest for immortality.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Take Me Out at the Ballgame
programme.desc: Find out how Murr chose which guys to honk at Volcano Bay, what happened to the TP used in the stock boy shootout, and how Joe stayed safe after messing with NY Mets fans at Citi Field.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Party Crasher
programme.desc: Find out which jokers were in on the surprise Gary Busey appearance, where the phrase 'tit for tat' came from, and how Murr's big mouth backfired on him, leading to this Statue of Liberty punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Marathon Man
programme.desc: Find out which joker has pet parakeets, why the crew had to work in a truck down the block while filming at a bagel shop and who got to sound the start horn for Murr's race across the Brooklyn Bridge.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Lords of the Ring
programme.desc: Find out which joker always brings a bag of dental hygiene products to set, why Q changed into cargo pants in a store window and where the art department found the turquoise ring.

programme.title: Hot Ones: The Game Show: Schooled by the Sauce
programme.desc: Two teachers get schooled by the fiery inferno of hot sauce, while a rapper and his manager sweat was they try to beat the heat.

programme.title: Hot Ones: Halle Berry
programme.desc: Find out how Halle Berry takes spicy food as she lets her competitive spirit loose and discusses topics such as Kanye lyrics, dog training and her iconic film roles.

programme.title: Hot Ones: The Game Show: Worst. Hot Sauce. Ever
programme.desc: Two comic book guys go against two twerkin' besties. The 'Constrictor Sauce' knocks the comic book guys to their knees, but the keep fighting for the prize.

programme.title: Hot Ones: The Game Show: TKOMG
programme.desc: Cocky sisters challenge best bros in the Pepperdome. However, the sauce proves too hot to handle for one team, resulting in the first TKO.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Needy and the Greedy
programme.desc: A behind the scenes look at the location that refused to let the Jokers film, Joe's injury from being thrown into a tower of cereal and why Q was called a 'loose cannon'.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Like a Boss
programme.desc: Why the slow-moving line at The Fast and the Furious ride made things harder for the guys, who officiated Joe's wedding, and why last-minute changes were needed for Murr's Tumblr punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Down in the Dump
programme.desc: Find out what objects other than pencils the Jokers have considered putting on people, and whose sister frequently helps on the show. Plus, learn what prop has become a fan favorite.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Enter the Dragons
programme.desc: Find out which Joker's dad has appeared on the show, and which natural disaster affected one of the challenges. Plus, learn how Sal came to own a replica of Van Halen's guitar.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: Whodunit?
programme.desc: Councilwoman Price tells Terry to find the author of an anonymous firefighter blog from the station and shut it down. With everyone a suspect, no one can be trusted.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: Talkoma: Whodunit?
programme.desc: Kevin Heffernan and Steve Lemme take viewers behind the scenes in after show filled with cast interviews, and bonus footage.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Card Against Humanity
programme.desc: Find out which Jokers really wear glasses, how Q was injured the last time he wore his talking brush disguise and what Sal's sole business card actually says.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Bull Shiatsu
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes to find out what word Joe uses as slang for cash, which prop did Q have to handle with care and how Joe managed to keep cool during his chair punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Pay It Forward
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes on the very first Impractical Jokers episode and discover what social superpower the Jokers now possess. Plus, find out which audio equipment is not used anymore and the true history around Joe's 'nosing'.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Enter the Dragons
programme.desc: Find out which Joker's dad has appeared on the show, and which natural disaster affected one of the challenges. Plus, learn how Sal came to own a replica of Van Halen's guitar.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: Whodunit?
programme.desc: Councilwoman Price tells Terry to find the author of an anonymous firefighter blog from the station and shut it down. With everyone a suspect, no one can be trusted.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: Talkoma: Whodunit?
programme.desc: Kevin Heffernan and Steve Lemme take viewers behind the scenes in after show filled with cast interviews, and bonus footage.

programme.title: Hot Ones: The Game Show: A Hot Mess
programme.desc: Fiery identical twins take on saucy drag queens in the Pepperdome. It's duke it out or puke it out in this episode where one player becomes very intimate with the Hot Ones' spit bucket.

programme.title: Hot Ones: The Game Show: No Sugar, Tons of Spice
programme.desc: Two teams duke it out against the catastrophic concoctions from Chef Willie.

programme.title: Hot Ones: John Mayer
programme.desc: John Mayer takes on the wings of death and everything else along the way, including Justin Bieber's melody wizardry and the best guitar faces in history.

programme.title: Hot Ones: Kumail Nanjiani
programme.desc: Kumail Nanjiani discusses tech bros, breaks down his favourite video games, and explains his obsession with 'The X-Files.' Not only that, but he becomes the first guest in the show's history to dine with a knife and fork.

programme.title: Hack My Life: Hack to the Future
programme.desc: Go to the future with Kevin as he lives out his hoverboard dream, and watch Brooke and Kevin spot a hack that helps your sheets feel like a million bucks.

programme.title: Hack My Life: To Tell a Lie
programme.desc: Brooke and Kevin meet their toughest challenge yet when they use hacks to game a polygraph test, and Kevin learns how to defend himself with a smartphone.

programme.title: Hack My Life: The Hack's Out the Bag
programme.desc: Kevin and Brooke battle for the best kitten hacks and test user-submitted hacks to bring dead batteries back to life. Plus, Brooke hacks a pizza in a waffle iron and we find out how the cheeseburger became a 'Hack from History.'

programme.title: Hack My Life: Hitting the Hackpot
programme.desc: Kevin and Brooke test the old banana in the tailpipe hack from a wild 80's movie. Kevin hacks a fancy chandelier out of plastic spoons, then he spills the tea on hacks that casinos use to hustle you.

programme.title: TruTV's Top Funniest: Outrageous Antics
programme.desc: Learn the meaning of 'car trouble' in this collection of four wheel fails. Watch a snoozing frat boy get a rude awakening, and find out how a horse snuck its way into a family wedding album.

programme.title: TruTV's Top Funniest: Frolics and Follies
programme.desc: Watch a group of heavy metal rockers turn into extreme home decorators. Also, a forklift, a car and a seesaw come together in an epic stunt. Plus, women turn the tables on men with some side-splitting pranks.

programme.title: TruTV's Top Funniest: Happy Fun Times
programme.desc: Watch kids turn the tables on their parents with some outrageous pranks; a beach bum takes a ride down a water slide, in the buff and a daredevil does a second-story dive into a shopping mall fountain.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: Thrillseekers 6
programme.desc: An alligator trainer who's almost alligator lunch, a clumsy cliff diver, and an amateur stunt man who will probably never go pro.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Thrillseekers 7
programme.desc: Thrill seekers are captured in their less than fine moments, from flying cars to bikes with two bicyclists.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: Thrillseekers 8
programme.desc: Stars offer hilarious commentaries while viewing footage of the world's dumbest people.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Dover and Out
programme.desc: The guys survey employees with odd questions, and tonight's loser unveils a mural in a small town whose residents react with big city rage.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: No Good Deed
programme.desc: Find out what after-school activity Joe's mom forbid him from doing, why filming nearly halted during the jack asses challenge and what last minute idea made it into Sal and Joe's charity funds punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Stripteased
programme.desc: Find out what prop Q asked to keep from the dental damn challenge, what glazed delight is known to give Joe great power and how a doctor's appointment inspired Murr's striptease punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Running of the Bullies
programme.desc: Find out which Joker's waiter experience made meal or no meal a breeze, why there was a white noise machine needed during dust bunnies and who issued a siren warning right after Sal's pool punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Ash Clown
programme.desc: The guys give customers an eye opening experience at a glasses store, and convince customers to pass illicit, and ridiculous, messages while shopping for groceres.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Centaur of Attention
programme.desc: The guys go head-to-head in a focus group, and then spread some confusion at a bagel store before heading to the park where they hit all the wrong notes. Finally, tonight's biggest loser gets hunted down during a truly humiliating walk of shame.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Hump Day
programme.desc: Learn how the crew attracted more customers to eat at GRK, what piece of iconic 'Jokers' history was made in this same focus group space and who caused several delays in the filming of Q's zoo punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Enter the Dragons
programme.desc: Find out which Joker's dad has appeared on the show, and which natural disaster affected one of the challenges. Plus, learn how Sal came to own a replica of Van Halen's guitar.

programme.title: Movie: Monster-in-Law
programme.desc: Viola Field's fall from grace as a successful television journalist has left her looking for control in her life. When her son announces his engagement to a working-class woman, Viola sets out to ruin her son's marriage before it gets started.

programme.title: Movie: Maid in Manhattan
programme.desc: A senatorial candidate falls for a hotel maid who is posing as a wealthy Manhattan socialite.

programme.title: One World: Together at Home

programme.title: Suits: Full Disclosure
programme.desc: Harvey learns the scope of Alex's involvement in the prison case, while Mike races to keep it alive. Lipschitz forces Louis out of his comfort zone. Donna struggles with her work/life balance.

programme.title: Suits: 100
programme.desc: Harvey and Mike seek outside help to overcome an impossible situation. An unexpected encounter derails Louis's search for a new associate. Donna receives a surprising proposition.

programme.title: Suits: Shame
programme.desc: Mike proposes a power move to boost business, but Harvey's past complicates matters. Rachel is surprised when her dad offers a chance to join forces. Louis mentors Brian.

programme.title: Suits: Donna
programme.desc: Mike and Harvey's power move is countered, putting their backs up against the ropes. Rachel fears her father's judgment is clouded by the past. Louis aids Alex when his client comes under fire.

programme.title: Suits: Hard Truths
programme.desc: Harvey and Louis fight to protect the future of the firm from its past. Alex worries Mike's instincts could risk a client relationship. Donna and Harvey delve into the aftermath of their kiss.

programme.title: Suits: Bad Man
programme.desc: Harvey has to call in an old debt for the firm. Mike goes toe-to-toe with an unlikely opponent. Louis comes to term with his new role.

programme.title: Suits: Inevitable
programme.desc: Harvey is forced into a difficult decision when the impossible is asked of him. Mike and Rachel try to make time to discuss their future. Louis struggles with the rules of his relationship.

programme.title: Suits: Pulling the Goalie
programme.desc: Mike's attempt to distract Harvey with a case backfires. Louis tries not to let emotion affect his judgment in a legal duel. Donna finds a way to prove her worth to the firm.

programme.title: Suits: Tiny Violin
programme.desc: Harvey and Louis are blindsided by an attack against Specter Litt. Meanwhile, Mike can't turn his back on the clinic when a class action suit overwhelms Nathan and Oliver.

programme.title: Suits: Good-Bye
programme.desc: It's all hands on deck as Louis, Donna, and Alex defend the firm, while Harvey aids Jessica on a case. Mike and Rachel juggle their commitments to the firm, the clinic and each other.

programme.title: Movie: The Wedding Planner
programme.desc: A workaholic wedding planner falls for the engaged doctor whose wedding she is organizing.

programme.title: Movie: What to Expect When You're Expecting
programme.desc: Five interconnected couples come to understand love and that life does not always deliver what you expect.

programme.title: Movie: Monster-in-Law
programme.desc: Viola Field's fall from grace as a successful television journalist has left her looking for control in her life. When her son announces his engagement to a working-class woman, Viola sets out to ruin her son's marriage before it gets started.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: I for Insane Ice Cream
programme.desc: Guy Fieri rolls out for ice cream, Triple D style. At the Norfolk, Virginia drive-in where they say their founder invented the waffle cone in the early 1900s and they're still making them by hand today.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: J for Jet-Setting Dishes
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's taking on some truly unique eats. In Honolulu, the Jamaican joint serving' up jerk chicken wings and some wicked goat curry. Then, in Arlington, Texas, visit the Russian spot flipping a potato pancake stuffed with beef.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: K for Killer Burgers
programme.desc: This trip, Guy is rolling out for the all-American favourite, and then some. In Kansas City, Missouri, a pub where they're dipping burgers in tempura batter or putting them between a pair of grilled cheese sandwiches.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: L for Lights-Out Latin
programme.desc: This trip, Guy dives into all kinds of amazing Latin specialties. In Miami, Florida husband-and-wife team dishing out primo Peruvian sandwiches. In Wilmington, North Carolina, a funky joint cooking up the flavors of Panama.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: M for Mucho Mexican
programme.desc: Guy heads south of the border to dig into Cancun cuisine, like an authentic, family-owned spot where grandma's cooking up real-deal rellenos and killer chicharron gorditas. Plus, an inside look at Guy's Play del Carmen restaurant.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: N for NOLA Eats
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's taking a tasty tour of New Orleans, Louisiana. He rolls into a legit butcher shop with a funky take on the Big Easy's comfort food flavours and checks out a seafood spot getting uber-creative with their dishes.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: O for Off-the-Hook Eats
programme.desc: Guy Fieri checks out joints doing one-of-a-kind favourites from coast to coast. In Portland, Oregon, a BBQ place smoking trout and lamb ribs. Then, in Deerfield Beach, Florida a beach town pub is doing a new twist on Oysters Rockefeller.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: P for Piling On the Pork
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri is digging in and pigging out starting with a Puerto Rican spot in San Antonio that's building a unique take on nachos and cooking up a monster chop. In New Orleans, a gangster grill serves up pork knuckles and some sweet bird.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: Q for 'Que-licious
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's tucking in the napkin and getting into some righteous barbecue. In Dallas, the barbecue stand tucked into a farmers' market, dishing out classic beef brisket and Carolina pork. He also visits a long-time meat market near Las Vegas.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: R for Real-Deal Route 66
programme.desc: Guy checks out places locals have loved for decades. In San Francisco, a classic joint that's made fresh-carved hot meat sandwiches for more than 60 years. In Omaha, an 80-year-old tavern has turned into a fish market.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: S for Shut-the-Front-Door Sandwiches
programme.desc: Guy digs into succulent sandwiches. In Oakland, real-deal butchery stacks up killer cheesesteak and off-the-chain chicken. In Modesto, California, a funky food truck puts pastrami on pretzel bread and cooking pulled pork in root beer.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: T for Triple D Tailgate
programme.desc: Guy Fieri hits a Raiders home game for tailgating with chefs from three local joints and Raiders quarterback Ken Stabler. They're bringing the kitchen to the blacktop and cranking out chicken and waffles, double chili cheeseburgers and steak sandwiches.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: U for Unreal Finds
programme.desc: Guy discovers some hidden gems on this trip. In Stone Harbor, New Jersey, a fish shack flying in Paco Paco fish from the Amazon for a Brazilian specialty. In Long Island City, New York, a 14-seat joint make wood oven pizza with sides.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: V for Viewers' Choice
programme.desc: This trip, Guy is going where viewers sent him. In Lexington, Kentucky, a restored 1951 drive-in is still serving the same double burger that made it famous. In Jacksonville, Florida, a diner in an old soda shop has a from scratch menu.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: W for Wings 'n' Things!
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri checks out some serious scratch cooking. In Washington, a soul food joint serving up chicken wings smothered in house made buffalo sauce, where he's joined by NFL player LaMont Jordan.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: X for Xtreme Cuisine
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's digging into some dishes like mama made, if she had a wild imagination. In Philadelphia, a very special guest, Marc Summers, hits the kitchen with Guy for fried PB&Js and tacos that start with the head of a pig.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: Y for You Made What?!?
programme.desc: Guy is hitting the road and taking a detour to find some out-of-the-ordinary eats. In Chico, California, a Hawaiian-Asian fusion spot serves ramen and a fried chicken bao. In Vancouver, British Columbia, a funky joint puts a spin on lamb and pork belly.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: Z for 'ZA
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieri is diving into the pie and other major mouthfuls. In Boston, a joint putting beer into their crust and then topping it with taco fixings and a steak-and-cola combo. Then, a restaurant serves up a mind-blowing BLT.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: A for Awesome Asian
programme.desc: Guy gets a big taste of the Far East in Las Vegas. He chows down on Japanese and Korean.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: B for Breakfast Out of Bounds
programme.desc: Guy Fieri searches out some of America's greatest breakfasts. Visit a restaurant that makes 30 different kinds of pancakes and another spot with 60 different omelettes.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: C for Cookin' Up Comfort
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's diving into all kinds of comfort cooking. In Phoenix, a funky joint stacking up barbecue fries and a new spin on chilaquiles. Plus, visit a Dallas spot cranking out all kinds of off-the-chain chicken.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A-Z: D for Dynamite Dough
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieri is digging into dishes featuring some dynamite dough. In Mesa, Arizona, a legit Hawaiian joint serving island specialties, including a meaty bun, and in Tucson, Arizona, a place cooking up a big-time saucy sandwich.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics: Cook Like a Pro: Perfect Pastry
programme.desc: Ina Garten shares tips and ideas for cooking with sweet, savory and even store-bought pastry. She starts with her foolproof sweet pastry dough for a showstopping French fig tart. She uses a shortcut to make spinach in puff pastry.

programme.title: Brunch @ Bobby's: Wake Up Your Sweet Tooth
programme.desc: Bobby Flay and guest Sunny Anderson tackle the sweet side of brunch with creme brulee French toast with drunken strawberries, Sunny's sambal and pineapple glazed bacon, blueberry pancake bites and sparkling iced espresso.

programme.title: Giada Entertains: Taste Of Capri Party
programme.desc: After a recent trip to Capri, Italy, Giada De Laurentiis hosts a lunch party for her girlfriends that celebrates the island and one of its most inspiring ingredients; the lemon.

programme.title: Valerie's Home Cooking: Home Alone... And I Like It
programme.desc: Valerie pulls together a quick meal after spending all day researching. She prepares smoky cheddar crackers, pasta al tonno, roasted artichoke hearts provencal, and blueberry cheesecake filo cups.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Breakfast Freezer Faves
programme.desc: It's a busy day on the ranch for Ree Drummond as she fills her freezer with breakfast favorites and makes breakfast for the guys out working cattle. Her to-make list includes freezer breakfast paninis and very berry croissants.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Lighter 16-Minute Meals
programme.desc: Ree cooks against the clock to make four of her favorite recipes over into lighter, healthier 16-minute meals.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Low-Carb Party Bites
programme.desc: Irresistible sausage stuffed mushrooms and make-ahead zucchini roll ups. Baked brie a la Ree and jalapeno cheese crisps. Finally, stunning salmon and cucumber bites and the simplest Spanish marinated olives.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Staying Home
programme.desc: It's a family affair as Ree Drummond shoots a very special episode with the help of her kids. Ree welcomes everyone to her quarantine kitchen and shares easy, pantry-inspired recipes for difficult times.

programme.title: Girl Meets Farm: Dad's Midwest Concert
programme.desc: Molly Yeh's dad is in town to play a clarinet concert, and she's making an Asian-inspired lunch of succulent sugar-coated pork buns and crunchy drunken cucumber noodles to fill him up beforehand.

programme.title: Valerie's Home Cooking: Kitchen Cousins
programme.desc: When Valerie Bertinelli's husband's cousins come to town, she is challenged with converting cousin Mary's favorite Italian recipes into healthier versions, so that Mary can still indulge while staying on a heart-healthy regimen.

programme.title: Delicious Miss Brown: Picnic in the Park
programme.desc: Kardea picnics in the park with friends and her favourite one-hand wonders like rotisserie chicken salad sandwiches with homemade bacon jam and chilled black-eyed pea salad. The lunch finishes with delicious blackberry hand pies.

programme.title: Giada Entertains: Earth Day Party
programme.desc: In honor of the 50th anniversary of Earth Day, Giada De Laurentiis shares a menu sourced completely from the garden and throws an eco-friendly party with all recycled products.

programme.title: The Kitchen: Going Green
programme.desc: The kitchen is honouring Earth Day with fresh finds for spring. Geoffrey Zakarian makes a Peruvian seafood stew with cilantro broth, and then Jeff Mauro goes green with his Italian stuffed artichokes. Katie Lee makes Asian-style eggplant 'meat' balls.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Express Lane Extreme: Name Your Number
programme.desc: Guy Fieri unveils the ultimate strategy game in which the four competing chefs must decide whether to use six, eight or ten ingredients before knowing what kind of dish they're making.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Big Budget Bonanza
programme.desc: Four penny-pinching pros who have braved some of Flavortown's toughest budget battles are back for a brutal budget bonanza. Guy Fieri's budget wheel determines the ingredients for their breakfast creations.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Last Judge Standing Tournament: Part 1
programme.desc: Eight Triple G judges compete for the title of Last Judge Standing and $40,000 for charity. Guy spins a wheel to determine whose least favorite ingredients will wind up in their steak house dinner.

programme.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia: Mac Fights Gay Marriage
programme.desc: Mac pulls out the big guns to protect the sanctity of marriage, while the rest of the gang reap the rewards of marital bliss.

programme.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia: Dennis Gets Divorced
programme.desc: Marital bliss turns to marital business, as the gang experiences the emotional and economic tribulations of divorce.

programme.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia: The Gang Buys a Boat

programme.title: You're the Worst: Four Goddamn More Days

programme.title: You're the Worst: We Were Having Such a Nice Day

programme.title: FXM Presents

programme.title: Movie: Suburbicon
programme.desc: When a house is burgled in the middle of the night, the idyllic 1950s community of Suburbicon is shocked to its core, especially when one of the victims is killed. As her husband deals with the aftermath, an insurance investigator becomes suspicious.

programme.title: Movie: Mission: Impossible - Rogue Nation
programme.desc: Ethan Hunt and his team of highly trained Impossible Missions Force operatives must seek out and destroy a rogue international assassin organization called the Syndicate that is hell-bent on taking the IMF down.

programme.title: The Simpsons: The Call of the Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer buys a beat-up motor home to take the family on a camping trip. But they quickly run into trouble when Homer gets lost and is mistaken for Big Foot.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Homer Alone
programme.desc: Marge takes a vacation from the family at Rancho Relaxo, leaving Homer by himself.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Brother, Can You Spare Two Dimes?
programme.desc: Herbert Powell returns and asks the Simpsons for financial help for an invention.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Rosebud
programme.desc: When Mr. Burns wants to get his favourite stuffed bear, Bobo, back from Maggie Simpson, he offers Homer one million dollars and three Hawaiian islands in exchange for the toy. Meanwhile, Smithers organizes a birthday party for Mr. Burns.

programme.title: The Simpsons: And Maggie Makes Three
programme.desc: Homer tells the story about how he had to give up his dream job after Maggie was born.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Hello Gutter, Hello Fadder
programme.desc: Homer bowls a perfect game and becomes a Springfield celebrity.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Moe Baby Blues
programme.desc: Maggie takes a liking to Moe when he saves her from accidentally falling off a bridge.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Smart and Smarter
programme.desc: Maggie is accepted into a gifted preschool, even though she has not yet said a word.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Gone Maggie Gone
programme.desc: When Homer leaves Maggie on the doorstep of a convent to keep her safe while rescuing Santa's Little Helper, the nuns at the convent take Maggie in. Lisa comes up with a plan to infiltrate the convent and rescue Maggie.

programme.title: The Simpsons: I'm Just a Girl Who Can't Say D'oh
programme.desc: Marge becomes Director of Springfield's local theater, armed with Lisa's script resembling 'Hamilton.' Meanwhile, Homer joins a baby class with Maggie, where he takes a liking to supervisor Chloe.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet: Asia
programme.desc: Visit the planet's largest continent. In the frozen north, walrus congregate in extraordinary numbers, bears gather inside volcanoes and yeti-like monkeys fight in the remote forests of China. In the tropical south, the last few Sumatran rhino roam.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet: Europe
programme.desc: Europe is where wildlife survives alongside people. In Gibraltar, a baby monkey is kidnapped, while in the cemeteries of Vienna grave robbing wild hamsters fight. In this fairy tale continent wolves roam the forests and dragons lurk in caves.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet: Antarctica
programme.desc: Only the strong can survive in Antarctica, the world's most hostile continent. Elephant seals battle for territories, king penguins gather in colonies, and great whales feast on krill bait.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet: Africa
programme.desc: Even in Africa, home of the greatest wildlife gatherings on Earth, wildlife faces many challenges.

programme.title: Life Story: Home
programme.desc: To survive, animals need somewhere to live, a place that provides the necessities of life, shelter from the elements and refuge from enemies.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet: Australia
programme.desc: A land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs. Here, kangaroos and wombats brave snowstorms and gum tree forests are filled with never-before-seen predators.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet: North America
programme.desc: In winter, lynx prowl the Yukon for snowshoe hares and manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze. In summer, Tennessee fish build spectacular underwater pyramids, fireflies light up the forest's nights, and polar bears leap from rocks as they hunt.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet: South America
programme.desc: A look at the most species rich continent on Earth: South America. In the Andes, pumas hunt guanaco while rarely seen bears search for mini avocados. In the Amazon, poison dart frogs care for their babies and colourful macaws eat clay.

programme.title: Life Story: Home
programme.desc: To survive, animals need somewhere to live, a place that provides the necessities of life, shelter from the elements and refuge from enemies.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet: Asia
programme.desc: Visit the planet's largest continent. In the frozen north, walrus congregate in extraordinary numbers, bears gather inside volcanoes and yeti-like monkeys fight in the remote forests of China. In the tropical south, the last few Sumatran rhino roam.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet: Europe
programme.desc: Europe is where wildlife survives alongside people. In Gibraltar, a baby monkey is kidnapped, while in the cemeteries of Vienna grave robbing wild hamsters fight. In this fairy tale continent wolves roam the forests and dragons lurk in caves.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet: Antarctica
programme.desc: Only the strong can survive in Antarctica, the world's most hostile continent. Elephant seals battle for territories, king penguins gather in colonies, and great whales feast on krill bait.

programme.title: Hidden Habitats: Yellowstone
programme.desc: In 1985, wolves were released back into Yellowstone National Park. The re-introduction of the predator has set off a cascade of events through the food chain.

programme.title: Hidden Habitats: Australia's Red Centre
programme.desc: The Red Center of Australia is one of the most inhospitable environments on Earth yet it is filled with wildlife. The secret to Australia's diversity is its rainforest past.

programme.title: Madagascar: Island of Marvels
programme.desc: Examine clues from Madagascar's animals, plants and landscape to discover how the island's past has produced its present; the Tsingy have cut off animals in isolated gorges allowing them to evolve unique species.

programme.title: Madagascar: Lost Worlds
programme.desc: The narrow eastern strip of the island is rich in life; travel from the highest mountains, where trees are few, to lush rainforests, to the tropical coast, home to ring-tailed lemurs, jewelled geckos and predatory wasps.

programme.title: Madagascar: Land of Heat and Dust
programme.desc: Some of the strategies for survival in Madagascar's dry south are ingenious.

programme.title: Killing Eve: Slowly Slowly Catchy Monkey
programme.desc: Eve is attempting to rebuild her life, having been shot by Villanelle. Villanelle is also moving on, until she is approached by an old foe. Carolyn is being undermined at work and Kenny can’t leave his own investigation into The Twelve alone.

programme.title: Movie: Underworld: Rise of the Lycans
programme.desc: Lucian, a young Lycan, becomes a powerful leader among the slave Lycans. He plans a rebellion against the vampires' rule with the support of his secret lover and vampire, Sonja.

programme.title: Movie: Underworld
programme.desc: A vampire warrior finds herself falling in love with a human who has been bitten by a werewolf, the sworn enemies of her clan.

programme.title: Movie: Underworld: Evolution
programme.desc: A werewolf and a vampire, forbidden lovers, attempt to unlock the secret behind their tribes' feud.

programme.title: The '80s: The Decade That Made Us: Masters of the Universe
programme.desc: With the motto 'greed is good,' Gordon Gekko desires excess and ambition in 'Wall Street' while traders mirror his bad behaviour in real life. CEOs see their salaries rise while women fight to overcome office boys' clubs.

programme.title: The '80s: The Decade That Made Us: Tear Down These Walls
programme.desc: Though the 1980s were known for its 'greed is good' mantra, the decade also saw its fair share of literal and cultural walls brought down, as Americans united on political, racial and health issues.

programme.title: The '80s: The Decade That Made Us: Super Power
programme.desc: At the end of the 1980s American pop culture goes global and helps bring down the Iron Curtain, leaving America as the world's sole superpower.

programme.title: One World: Together at Home
programme.desc: This special, in support of the fight against the COVID-19 pandemic, will feature the real experiences from doctors, nurses and families around the world, and inspire people to take meaningful action to increase assistance for the COVID-19 response.

programme.title: The '80s: The Decade That Made Us: Lift Off
programme.desc: See how the '80s created our modern world including the revolution in personal entertainment brought on by Pac-Man and Sony Walkmans while a new kid on the block defined a new musical era - spelled 'MTV.'

programme.title: The '80s: The Decade That Made Us: The Revolutionaries
programme.desc: Reagan's business mandate leads to a new breed of entrepreneurs, including Steve Jobs, whose '1984' ad helped launch the Super Bowl commercial craze and Ted Turner, who invented the 24-hour news network.

programme.title: Drugs, Inc.: Hip Hop High
programme.desc: Hip-hop, a million-dollar industry, plays up to its links with a billion-dollar industry: drugs. In Oakland California, wannabe rappers BG and Lil Ant use money from dealing cocaine to pay for their studio time.

programme.title: Drugs, Inc.: Marijuana Mayhem
programme.desc: The rapidly growing appetite for California Sour Diesel, a potent strain of marijuana said to induce an instant high, is helping fuel a violent drug underworld that stretches from coast to coast.

programme.title: Drugs, Inc.: Motor City Rush
programme.desc: A failing city with wealthy suburbs, Detroit is a drug dealer's dream. White kids travel downtown to score and US Border Patrol Agents have taken over routine drug stops.

programme.title: Drugs, Inc.: Tex Meth
programme.desc: Dealers battle for center stage during festival time in the 'live music capital of the world,' Austin, Texas.

programme.title: Drugs, Inc.: Aussie Ice Wars
programme.desc: Outlaw bikers battle cops and Chinese Triads to keep control of the highly lucrative crystal meth trade.

programme.title: Drugs, Inc.: Pittsburgh Smack
programme.desc: As Pittsburgh’s cops crack down on heroin, wannabe dealers see a chance of muscling in on the market - inadvertently opening the door to a killer brand.

programme.title: Australia's Most Dangerous: Perilous Waters
programme.desc: Beware what lurks beneath the water's surface.

programme.title: Australia's Most Dangerous: Speed Demons
programme.desc: Featuring: the miniscule itchy grub; the platypus; and the mammoth water buffalo.

programme.title: Wild New Zealand: The Lost World
programme.desc: Discover New Zealand's most ancient and interesting species as we explore how this spectacular land was formed and how it's extreme isolation both protected and transformed these native creatures.

programme.title: Wild New Zealand: Middle Earth
programme.desc: Witness the ways in which New Zealand's resourceful and ingenious wildlife have adapted to survive.

programme.title: Wild Australia: Desert of the Red Kangaroo
programme.desc: In the heart of Australia lives the red kangaroo, the largest marsupial on earth and a true master of adapting to the blistering desert heat.

programme.title: Wild Australia: Jurassic Jungle
programme.desc: The Australian tropics are perhaps the most mysterious part of the country. Impenetrable rainforest hides kangaroos that live in trees and a colossal bird with dinosaur feet.

programme.title: Wild Australia: Realm of the Wombat
programme.desc: The hostile dry plains of southern Australia are home to some extraordinary and lethal animals including deadly ants, killer bunnies and the surprisingly vicious wombat.

programme.title: Wild Australia: Koala Forest
programme.desc: There is more to the lush woodland of Australia than meets the eye. Strange birds build elaborate nests and strange mammals take to the air, but it’s the not-so-cuddly koala who steals the show.

programme.title: Secrets of the Zoo: King of the Jungle
programme.desc: Staff members at Columbus Zoo and The Wilds are counting down the days until spring.

programme.title: Secrets of the Zoo: Dhole Lotta Love
programme.desc: A 44-year-old orangutan has the biggest birthday bash of the year at the zoo.

programme.title: NASA's Unexplained Files: Planet of the Metal Aliens
programme.desc: After a strange discovery confirms that life is possible on its surface, scientists begin to investigate if a nearby exoplanet could be home to an alien civilization.

programme.title: NASA's Unexplained Files: The Moon's Strange Secret
programme.desc: The last humans to walk on the moon discover something strange on the lunar surface. A NASA scientist investigates a strange phenomenon responsible for horrific mass deaths. A landmark NASA mission threatens to destroy the reputation of an American hero.

programme.title: NASA's Unexplained Files: Aliens in the Amazon
programme.desc: After Neil Armstrong's moonwalk, he led an expedition into the Amazon to find a legendary library that holds all knowledge of an advanced alien civilization. Experts ask if a close encounter on the moon's surface inspired Armstrong's mysterious quest.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Saturn’s Dark Secrets
programme.desc: New technology and the latest NASA space missions are unlocking Saturn’s darkest secrets, and using cutting-edge science, astronomers investigate the strange things that happen on this planet and in its mysterious rings.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Did the Big Bang Really Happen?
programme.desc: New discoveries are causing astronomers to question if the Big Bang really happened, and using the latest science, they investigate if it wasn't just the start of our universe but many mysterious multiverses.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Cassini's Final Secrets
programme.desc: For twenty years, NASA's Cassini spacecraft revealed the strange secrets of Saturn until it vaporized in its atmosphere in a blaze of glory. Today its legacy lives on, as fresh data from the probe helps scientists make brand-new discoveries.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Uranus and Neptune: Rise of the Ice Giants
programme.desc: Uranus and Neptune are mysterious, icy worlds at the edge of our solar system, and new discoveries reveal that these strange planets might have helped start life on Earth.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Did the Big Bang Really Happen?
programme.desc: New discoveries are causing astronomers to question if the Big Bang really happened, and using the latest science, they investigate if it wasn't just the start of our universe but many mysterious multiverses.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Saturn
programme.desc: Saturn's secrets are out. The ferocious weather, the evolving ring system and the discovery of active geology on Saturn's moons has rewritten the textbooks. Scientists are looking for life on Saturn's moons and they may have found it on Titan.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: How Black Holes Made Us
programme.desc: New discoveries reveal that Black holes might have been essential to creating stars, giving light, and building the universe itself, and aren't the violent monsters we think they are.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Dark History of the Solar System
programme.desc: Our solar system hides a dark and violent past, and new discoveries reveal that Earth and the planets were formed from the destruction of strange alien worlds that came before us.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Asteroid Apocalypse: The New Threat
programme.desc: Explore the inner workings of outer space and how the earth was created.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Twin Suns: The Alien Mysteries
programme.desc: Planets that orbit two suns instead of one might be deadly hell worlds, but new discoveries reveal that sci-fi star systems with binary stars might be optimal places for alien life.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Death of the Last Stars
programme.desc: Our universe's stars are dying off faster than new ones are born, and using the latest technology, experts investigate the secrets of the last stars of the cosmos and what this stellar apocalypse means for life on earth.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: When Supernovas Strike
programme.desc: Supernovas are the violent death of giant stars, and new discoveries reveal that these cataclysmic events create the elements that are essential to all life in the universe.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Earth's Death Orbit
programme.desc: Earth's journey through the universe is a perilous one. New discoveries reveal that our planet is heading toward a mysterious area of the cosmos that could eject us out of the Milky Way and into oblivion.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Secrets of Time Travel
programme.desc: Time travel might be more possible than we ever imagined, and today, cutting-edge technology and the latest research into black holes and wormholes is helping experts get steps closer to discovering how to travel through time.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Hunt for Alien Life
programme.desc: The latest discoveries suggest that we might be on the verge of discovering life beyond our planet, and scientists are investigating if earth's life began elsewhere in the universe, and whether we need to evolve to know for sure.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Are Black Holes Real?
programme.desc: New discoveries challenge everything we know about black holes, and astronomers question if they even exist; the latest science tries to explain how they work and what they look like.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: The Quasar Enigma
programme.desc: Quasars are the brightest and most powerful objects in our universe, and though they have shaped the cosmos, they might ultimately destroy everything that exists.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Hunt for Alien Evidence
programme.desc: The discovery of extraterrestrial life might face an impossible challenge: the physics of the universe itself. Using cutting-edge technology, experts might be on the verge of a groundbreaking find and the evidence could already be in hand.

programme.title: Crime 360: Deadly Secrets
programme.desc: In Cleveland, Ohio, lead homicide detective Wally Everett and his partner Mike Smith are called out to a crime scene where a body lays hidden between some hedges and the victim's grandmother's house.

programme.title: Crime 360: Fatal Feud
programme.desc: Two men who live next to each other in a housing project get into a deadly argument. Crewell and Williams take two men's girlfriends back to headquarters to find out what happened.

programme.title: Crime 360: Trial by Fire
programme.desc: Det. Mocello responds to the scene of a suspicious fire in the bedroom of a first-floor apartment. He soon discovers that the deceased victim's charred body has sustained serious trauma.

programme.title: Crime 360: Gilpin Court Shooting
programme.desc: Jaime is called to a possible homicide; learned victim ran 100 yards before dying. The victim banged on a couple of doors crying for help before falling down and dying.

programme.title: Crime 360: Overkill
programme.desc: A 49-year-old male was brutally stabbed 46 times and left to die with a blanket covering him.

programme.title: Crime 360: At Death's Door
programme.desc: It's a race against time to identify a murder suspect before he disappears for good.

programme.desc: A gun battle breaks out in a quiet Cleveland suburb causing chaos for the neighbourhood. In the morning, a young father of two is found dead in his bedroom, and a single bullet may help to catch the killer.

programme.title: Jacked: Auto Theft Task Force: Devil on One Shoulder

programme.title: Jacked: Auto Theft Task Force: I Just Wanted My Burger
programme.desc: The A.T.T.F. is on the prowl in Newark, New Jersey and they have plenty to keep them busy. First they get a work out when a perp takes off running, later they recover a stolen tractor trailer and a stolen car at a drive-thru window.

programme.title: Jacked: Auto Theft Task Force: It's Job Security

programme.title: Web of Lies: The Deadly Hoax
programme.desc: When a tech-savvy computer whiz fails to make the grade as a pro video gamer, he becomes obsessed with finding internet infamy. What starts as an online prank spirals into a new, deadly crime called 'swatting' that chills the nation.

programme.title: Web of Lies: Searching for Mr. Wrong
programme.desc: Nikki McPhatter has a busy work and social life, but there's only one thing missing true love. When she doesn't show up for her usual work shift, her friends and family immediately worry -- and point to her online relationships.

programme.title: The Murderous Mr. Handcuffs
programme.desc: When Laura Simonson, a 37-year-old loving mother from Farmington, Minnesota, is left devastated by the death of her teenage daughter, she seeks comfort in the darkest corners of online dating and meets the man who will seal her fate.

programme.title: Deadly Legacy: Bill Bundy
programme.desc: Detective Jason Moran re-opens the case of serial killer John Wayne Gacy. Unbelievably, eight of his 33 victims were unidentified. The grieving family of Bill Bundy, missing for 40 years, wonder if he might have been a victim.

programme.desc: When Laura Simonson, a 37-year-old loving mother from Farmington, Minnesota, is left devastated by the death of her teenage daughter, she seeks comfort in the darkest corners of online dating and meets the man who will seal her fate.

programme.title: Dead Silent: The Curse of Dismal Creek
programme.desc: Sean Farmer travels to a remote campsite in Virginia for a fishing trip with his friend Scott Johnston. When a mysterious stranger appears, the area's dark past returns to haunt the campers.

programme.title: Dead Silent: Secrets of the Santa Cruz Mountains
programme.desc: When 19-year-old Linda Jozavich vanishes from work one night in 1979, it just doesn't make sense to her Santa Cruz community. Years later, the murder of another local teen, Lois Sigala, sends investigators a step closer to solving Linda's case.

programme.title: Evil Stepmothers: Selfish Desires
programme.desc: Rebecca Osborne appears charming as she pursues widower Jerry Barker, but disaster is in store for her new groom. Jerry's children fear the worst when they uncover their stepmother's dark secret, which alludesto what's to come for their loving dad.

programme.title: Evil Stepmothers: Deadly Role Play
programme.desc: Anita Smithey appears trapped in an abusive marriage, until Robert Cline comes along. After a fresh start with his family, Anita seems to feel safe. But she soon reveals a dark side to her new step kids. And the prey becomes the ultimate predator.

programme.title: Evil Stepmothers: Bloody Betrayal
programme.desc: Growing up with a disfigured face, Patti Donahue feels like the butt of every joke. As an adult, Patti vows to get the last laugh. And when she marries an innocent father of three, she turns their lives into a world of deception, abuse and murder.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Green-eyed Monsters
programme.desc: For these heartless women envy is the deadliest sin. An obsessed mistress sacrifices her lover's wife; a young lover kills her girlfriend to keep her from the arms of another; and an angry cowgirl destroys her ex's life by destroying his family.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Lover's Revenge
programme.desc: When these women were betrayed, they demanded payback. A crazed mother took a custody battle too far, a scorned lover wiped out her competition, and a sex addict stepped on everyone in her way.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Kill the Competition
programme.desc: In the game of love the stakes are high not everyone can be a winner.

programme.title: Torn From the Headlines: New York Post Reports: Last Call at the Falls
programme.desc: When the nude body of a beautiful young woman is found wrapped in a blanket on the side of a desolate East New York parkway, investigators find themselves in a race against the clock to find her psychotic killer before he strikes again.

programme.title: Torn From the Headlines: New York Post Reports: The Tuxedo King
programme.desc: When a prominent businessman is kidnapped from a diner parking lot for a multimillion dollar ransom, the NYPD races against the clock to find him alive. Meanwhile, a New York Post reporter uncovers a career making scoop- that could come at a fatal price.

programme.title: Torn From the Headlines: New York Post Reports: Baby Hope
programme.desc: A grisly discovery in a picnic cooler on a highway in upper Manhattan leads detectives down a twisting trail of clues as they try to solve one of the longest-running mysteries in the New York Post's history, and hunt down a deranged child killer.

programme.title: Torn From the Headlines: New York Post Reports: The Blood Will Not Rest
programme.desc: A crime caught on tape shows Hasidic landlord Menachem Stark in the fight for his life. When his body is found the next day, the NYPD is on the hunt for his killers - leading them to potentially deadly secrets about the beloved Brooklyn landlord.

programme.title: The Lake Erie Murders: Devil at the Crossroads
programme.desc: 11-year-old Shauna Howe disappears as she returns home from a girl scouts Halloween party. When Shauna's body shows up in a local creek, her murder ignites an investigation, whose conclusion will send shockwaves through her community.

programme.title: Ultimate Warfare: Hue: Vietnam's Bloodiest Battle
programme.desc: In a final showdown marines must breech the heavily fortified walls of the ancient citadel where thousands of NVA soldiers lie in wait. After one of the bloodiest battles of the entire Vietnam War, the marines are finally victorious.

programme.title: The Mafia Kingpin
programme.desc: Arnold Rothstein schooled the founders of the American Mafia in the ways of organized crime during the 1920s and the the model for running crime business.

programme.title: The Last Don
programme.desc: Joe Massino, the 'Last Don', crushes anyone who goes against the mafia code of omerta. Loyalty to his family, and obedience to their code of silence, is everything. But when the Feds take Massino down, Massino takes everyone down with him.

programme.title: Mafia's Greatest Hits: Santo Trafficante
programme.desc: Florida and Cuba were ruled by top don Santo Trafficante. The King of Cuba's casinos was on the verge of mob stardom when a revolution destroyed his plans. It results in a secret alliance between the mob and the CIA and a failed assassination attempt.

programme.title: Mafia's Greatest Hits: Downfall
programme.desc: The FBI pursues the five families that run organized crime in New York, and what happens when the fate of the two informers is revealed.

programme.title: Mafia's Greatest Hits: Joey Gallo
programme.desc: Crazy Joey Gallo psychopathic killer, beatnik hipster and renegade mobster. Burning with rage and determined to make his way to the top, he wages all-out war against the US gov't and sparks a revolution in the mafia before going down in a hail of bullets.

programme.title: The Mafia Kingpin
programme.desc: Arnold Rothstein schooled the founders of the American Mafia in the ways of organized crime during the 1920s and the the model for running crime business.

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War: Fall of France
programme.desc: First-hand accounts of the incredible sacrifices and momentous feats endured by the Allies alongside the devastating consequences of the German capture of France.

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War: Battle of Britain
programme.desc: The British begin to face the Germans over the buildings of London and other British towns, and brave young pilots give their all for the Ally cause.

programme.title: Greatest Tank Battles: Tank Battles of Korea
programme.desc: In 1950 the world is caught off guard as North Korea launches a massive armoured assault into South Korea. This is the story of the American tankers who rush to the aid of the South and the bloody battles they fight up and down the Korean Peninsula.

programme.title: Against the Odds: The Battle for Sugar Loaf
programme.desc: Taking an otherwise nondescript hill on the embattled island of Okinawa proved to be one of the most incredible battles of WWII. The young weary marines of Charlie Company strained against the searing Pacific sun to see the top of Sugar Loaf Hill.

programme.title: Against the Odds: The Heroes of Tarawa
programme.desc: Everything went wrong in America's first test of whether the Japanese-held islands of the Pacific, the key steppingstones to the doorstep of Japan, could be captured by across-the-beach infantry assaults.

programme.title: Against the Odds: The Marines at Hue
programme.desc: The city of Hue, South Vietnam, was the site of one of the fiercest battles of the Vietnam War. Three under-strength U.S. Marine battalions, consisting of fewer than 2,500 men, attacked and soundly defeated overwhelming, entrenched enemy troops.

programme.title: Evolution of Evil: Hitler: The Benchmark of Terror
programme.desc: Adolf Hitler murders six million Jews and invades peaceful countries brutally killing anyone in his way. Find out what could turn a high school dropout into the most evil dictator the world has ever seen.

programme.title: Evolution of Evil: Saddam: The Butcher of Baghdad
programme.desc: The story of Saddam Hussein, the sadistic tyrant who ruled Iraq with an utter contempt for humanity for 23 years, a man whose cruelty knows no limits and who'll stop at nothing to achieve the power of life and death over millions of people.

programme.title: Evolution of Evil: Tojo: Japan's Razor of Fear
programme.desc: Japanese General, Hideki Tojo, is a cold blooded control freak, who takes millions to their deaths in the dirtiest war ever known. This is the story of the dictator responsible for Pearl Harbor, an Asian Holocaust and the torture of thousands of POWs.

programme.title: Evolution of Evil: Kim: North Korea's Evil Dynasty
programme.desc: North Korea's evil dictator, Kim Jong-il, ruled his country for 17 years without mercy. While personally enriching himself, he let his people starve to death and threatened to bring the world to the brink of a third world war.

programme.title: Hunting Nazi Treasure: Goering's Greed
programme.desc: Billions of dollars of art and treasure was stolen by the second most powerful figure in Nazi Germany. The team search for items stolen from Holland, including sunken treasure and lost masterpieces.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: A Dream Come True
programme.desc: A couple with five children calls on the Cabin Masters to take their newly purchased camp on David Pond and turn it into a family vacation cabin that will serve as a central part of their lives for generations to come.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Veterans' Retreat
programme.desc: A man is building a retreat to help combat-injured veterans and their families adjust to their new normal. The team are tasked with turning a cottage into an ADA-compliant event center. The key feature will be a deck that can accommodate up to 40.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Oyster Farm Shack
programme.desc: Ashley Morrill and her team of builders find themselves rebuilding an old oyster shack for a working oyster farm in Edgecomb, Maine.

programme.title: Insane Pools: Off the Deep End: Piña Pool-ada
programme.desc: Lucas creates a tropical oasis complete with an island, a large grotto rock and a Tiki-themed cabana.

programme.title: Insane Pools: Off the Deep End: Waterpark Wonderland
programme.desc: Pool genius Lucas transforms a neglected backyard into a waterpark wonderland. With his most extreme remodel yet, Lucas creates a family fun zone, complete with waterslide and double decker outdoor party space. But it's not all fun 'n games.

programme.title: Insane Pools: Off the Deep End: Dream Mulligan
programme.desc: Lucas is tasked with a challenging project: repair and remodel an unusable pool for a famous golfing family. Puzzled as to how this pool was even built, Lucas creates an ocean-themed pool with fire features, an infinity edge and his first water couch.

programme.title: Insane Pools: Off the Deep End: Rockin' it in Rocket City
programme.desc: Lucas goes to Huntsville, Alabama to build the lifelong dream pool of a retiring military couple. Lucas designs the largest grotto enclosed spa and widest waterfall of his career. This build also requires a tornado shelter.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: New York City
programme.desc: New York City is a melting pot of various ethnicities and culinary fusion. Andrew explores the iconic foods from the Big Apple like mile-high pastrami sandwiches, bagels and lox, pizza by the slice and the American hamburger.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Door County
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern scopes out the down-to-earth culinary traditions of scenic Door County, Wisconsin. Fresh fish from Lake Michigan and local produce defines the Nordic-influenced cuisine of this vacation getaway for nature lovers.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Florence
programme.desc: Florence, Italy lives up to its artistic reputation even in its food. Andrew Zimmern explores the classics like the rare Florentine steak, thick ribollita soup, the original cappuccino and the world famous frozen dessert, gelato.

programme.desc: In-depth analysis on prospective draft choices and breakdown of teams needs.

programme.title: Caught in the Draft: 1975: Agents of Change
programme.desc: In a draft that would be remembered for Walter Payton, top pick Steve Bartkowski sets the NFL world afire by asking his college dorm-mate Leigh Steinberg to negotiate his contract.

programme.title: 30 for 30: Elway to Marino
programme.desc: Explores the landmark 1983 NFL draft through the eyes of the players, head coaches, general managers, team owners and agents who participated including Marvin Demoff, who represented both John Elway and Dan Marino.

programme.title: Caught in the Draft: 1984
programme.desc: George Orwell's novel looms over the proceedings and the NFL engages in a war with the upstart USFL. Maryland's Boomer Esiason slips precipitously in the draft and BYU's Steve Young receives an offer he can't refuse.

programme.title: Caught in the Draft: 1985: Schemes and Dreams
programme.desc: Bill Walsh trades up with the New England Patriots and lands wide receiver Jerry Rice, but former Bengals head coach Sam Wyche reveals Walsh's frantic efforts to instead trade for the top-billed receiver in the draft, Eddie Brown.

programme.title: Caught in the Draft: 1994: A Day That Will Live in Infamy
programme.desc: Against the backdrop of highly touted quarterbacks Trent Dilfer and Heath Shuler jockeying for position, a verbal spat between draftnik Mel Kiper, Jr. and Bill Tobin of the Indianapolis Colts turns the Draft into must see television.

programme.title: Caught in the Draft: 1995: Expansion
programme.desc: Despite wowing at the NFL scouting combine, workout warrior Mike Mamula struggles on the field. Highly-touted running back Kijana Carter has a disappointing career, while draft afterthoughts Terrell Davis and Curtis Martin become stars.

programme.title: The Brady 6
programme.desc: A look back at Tom Brady's selection in the 6th round of the 2000 NFL Draft and the six quarterbacks taken before him.

programme.title: Caught in the Draft: 2005: How Low Will Aaron Go?
programme.desc: Aaron Rodgers drops in the draft while waiting in the Green Room, while former New York Giants general manager Ernie Accorsi enjoys his best draft ever.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Mayan Misery
programme.desc: Two survivalists are challenged to survive in a flooded jungle of Belize. The man and woman must face a labyrinth of Mayan caves filled with poisonous snakes and rabid bats.

programme.title: Botched: Here Comes Tummy Boo Hoo

programme.title: Botched: Foreign Bodies

programme.title: Botched: Totally Waist-ed
programme.desc: Dr. Nassif helps a man who just wants his ears back, and Dr. Dubrow hopes to fix a botched butt implant. Afterward, the doctors meet with a woman who has had 17 surgeries to look like the perfect pixie.

programme.title: Botched: Supa Fupa
programme.desc: Terry operates on a cancer survivor whose gynecologist did her tummy tuck, Paul performs a reconstructive rhinoplasty on a woman who's been teased about her nose since childhood, and a Brazilian human seeks the doctors' help for botched chest implants.

programme.title: Botched: The Boob Fountain of Youth
programme.desc: A drag queen seeks Dr. Dubrow's expertise to rejuvenate his old breast implants (and career). Meanwhile, Dr. Nassif tackles a severe nose deformity, and later, the doctors meet a woman who wants to blow up her butt!

programme.title: Botched: Seeing Double
programme.desc: A fitness enthusiast and nasal spray addict wants Paul to fix her nose, and a woman with deflated boobs comes to Terry for help. Later, the doctors meet the world's most identical twins.

programme.title: Botched: Pinched Perfect
programme.desc: Paul helps a singer find his voice again with a revision rhinoplasty, and Terry reconstructs the chest of a young mother whose surgeon put in two different sized implants. Later, the doctors assist a woman with stretched earlobes.

programme.title: Botched: Went to Mexico and Got Terrible Rhinorrhea
programme.desc: Dr. Nassif helps a mother of three out of a snotty situation, while Dr. Dubrow transforms a woman whose ex-boyfriend pressured her to get extremely large implants. Later, we meet a man from Kentucky who wants the King of Pop's infamous nose.

programme.title: Botched: Plastic Fantastic
programme.desc: After almost dying during a procedure in Ecuador, an ex-Playboy model wants a second chance to fix her neck and nose. Terry tries to help a nurse with one big boob instead of two, and reality star Angelique Morgan wants to be even more plastic.

programme.title: Botched: Double D-isaster
programme.desc: Paul helps a female impersonator who isn't comfortable walking the streets without makeup. A woman with 'sideshow boobs' goes under the knife to help her marriage, and the doctors extract silicone star implants from a former body-modification enthusiast.

programme.title: Botched: Short Changed at the Nipple Bank

programme.title: Botched: Man Boobs
programme.desc: Dr. Dubrow helps a mother who looks like she's been pregnant for 12 years, Dr. Nassif takes on a woman who hasn't been able to close her mouth since college and the doctors meet with a gambler with a serious case of man boobs.

programme.title: Botched: J-Cup Juggernaut
programme.desc: Dr. Nassif transforms a woman whose nose has been stuck at a '7 o'clock' angle, and Dr. Dubrow helps a patient get her groove back by fixing her man-boobs. Later, a woman comes from across the pond for help with her J-sized breasts.

programme.title: Botched: All Twerk and No Play
programme.desc: Dr. Dubrow helps a woman with boobs gone bad get her sexy back and a bodybuilder get back in the gym. Later, the doctors offer advice to a patient on her cheeky trans-formation.

programme.title: Botched: The Boobinati
programme.desc: Extra-large areolas force a sugar baby with 1600cc breasts to consult with the doctors. They also help a clumsy wife finally feel deserving of her husband by tacking her tricky nose.

programme.title: Botched: Silicone Slip-Ups
programme.desc: Ravaged by a flesh eating disease, a young woman needs help to save what's left of her breasts while a southern woman wants her witchy nose gone. A world famous drag queen seeks help for some silicone slip ups.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Last Judge Standing Tournament: Part 2
programme.desc: Judges combine numbers and letters while making a pasta dish with only eight ingredients beginning with the letter 's'. The bottom three chefs enter a wild card round where one must make a comfort food dish in an international cuisine style.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Last Judge Standing Tournament: Part 3
programme.desc: The six remaining judges are put into teams of two to make a regional seafood dish on a budget with a twist. The bottom two teams must each cook for themselves in the redemption round then elevate a guilty pleasure to make it to the semi-finals.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Last Judge Standing Tournament: Part 4
programme.desc: The remaining judges must create their best cheese dish while playing three of the hardest games. The winner heads to the finale and the remaining judges make a refined meal with ingredient from impromptu alley.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Last Judge Standing Tournament Finale: Judgment Day
programme.desc: The final four make a chicken dinner of the panelist's least favorite foods, then play a food pyramid game to determine ingredient weights and what item must be their dish. The final two make a meal of seven unusual ingredients to win $40,000 for charity.

programme.title: Buddy vs. Duff: Mini-Golf Cake

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: The Grill Fits the Bill
programme.desc: Food Network's Marc Murphy and Chef Anne Burrell want to rake Bobby Flay over the coals. They'll have help from two grill masters, Chef David Bancroft and BBQ champion Susie Bulloch, as they show no chill trying to grill Bobby.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: They Had Style, They Had Food, They Were There!
programme.desc: Actress Fran Drescher joins Chopped's Scott Conant to beat Bobby Flay with style, food and flair. They're upping the ante by bringing in two fantastic pastry chefs, Jessica Scott and Shelby Sieg, who are ready to bake Bobby into a corner.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Don't Drop The Beat (Bobby)!

programme.title: Movie: Underworld
